plugins.name: backup
plugins.description: On-demand and scheduled VM disk backup to S3/R2 — exports a stopped VM's disk, uploads it to any rclone remote, restores via CDI image-upload. Scheduled backups run entirely in-cluster via CronJobs. Run `corral backup create <vm> --dest r2:bucket/path` or `corral backup schedule <vm> --every 24h --keep 7 --to r2:bucket/path`.

plugins.name: bootc
plugins.description: Boot a bootable container image as a VM — bootc install runs on-cluster, then the VM kernel-boots from the image. create / images / rebuild / upgrade / switch / status. Run `corral bootc create <name> --image ...`.

plugins.name: snapsched
plugins.description: Scheduled VM snapshots with retention — a CronJob snapshots the VM per tick and prunes beyond --keep. Run `corral snapsched add <vm> --every 6h --keep 12`.

plugins.name: schedule
plugins.description: VM autostart/shutdown windows — CronJobs flip the VM's runStrategy on cron schedules. Run `corral schedule add <vm> --start "0 9 * * 1-5" --stop "0 18 * * 1-5"`.

plugins.name: gpu
plugins.description: GPU/PCI passthrough — discover device-plugin resources, permit them in the KubeVirt CR, attach to VMs. Run `corral gpu list`, `corral gpu attach <vm> --device amd.com/gpu`.

plugins.name: windows
plugins.description: First-class Windows VMs — UEFI + TPM + Hyper-V enlightenments, installer ISO via CDI, virtio-win driver CD-ROM, optional RDP. Run `corral windows create <name> --iso <url>`.

plugins.name: proxmox
plugins.description: Proxmox VE API compatibility layer — serve a /api2/json subset backed by KubeVirt, so Terraform providers and other Proxmox ecosystem tools can manage your VMs. Run `corral proxmox serve [--addr :8006]`.

plugins.name: vdi
plugins.description: Desktop pools (RFC-0001, Phase 1) — clone an already-built VM into a pool, assign members to users, connect. Run `corral vdi pool create <name> --from <golden-vm> --size N`, `corral vdi assign <pool> <user>`, `corral vdi connect <member>`. See docs/vdi.md for the full setup guide.
